frameworks.name: Affective Computing
frameworks.one_line: automatic recognition of emotional states from physiological signals

frameworks.name: Circumplex Model of Affect
frameworks.one_line: two-dimensional arousal-valence representation of emotional states

frameworks.name: Support Vector Machine classification
frameworks.one_line: supervised classifier mapping physiological features to affective quadrants

core_question: Can immersive virtual environments elicit distinct emotional states that are decodable from EEG and ECG dynamics?
key_assumptions: arousal and valence span emotion space
linear and nonlinear cardiac/neural dynamics carry affective information
immersive VR elicits stronger affect than non-immersive stimuli
manipulations: four virtual rooms tiling arousal-valence quadrants
key_findings: linear and nonlinear features from EEG and ECG support quadrant prediction
interpretations: immersive VR is a valid elicitor for affect recognition systems
combined neural and cardiac dynamics suffice to decode arousal-valence
